clips.title: Morning Coffee
clips.about: The clip shows a person making a cup of coffee in the morning, to teach simple present-tense action verbs for everyday routines.
lines: A woman pours coffee into a white mug.
'pours' is present simple, third person, so it ends in -s.
She adds a little milk to the cup.
'a little' goes before an uncountable noun like 'milk'.
She lifts the warm mug with her hand.
'warm' is an adjective and comes before the noun 'mug'.
She takes a slow sip and smiles.
'and' joins two actions in one sentence.

checks.q: What does the woman pour into the mug?
opts: She pours coffee from the pot into the mug.
The drink is coffee, not tea.
checks.why: The first line clearly says she pours coffee into the mug.

checks.q: What does the woman do at the end?
opts: She takes a sip and then smiles.
She smiles; she does not cry.
She is awake and drinking coffee, not sleeping.
checks.why: The last line shows her taking a sip and smiling.

clips.title: Buying an Apple
clips.about: The clip shows a person buying one apple at a fruit stall, to teach simple present-tense verbs and everyday shopping words.
lines: A man picks up one red apple.
'one' shows the number, so 'apple' is singular.
He gives a coin to the vendor.
'gives' is present simple, third person, ending in -s.
He puts the apple into a bag.
'into' shows movement to the inside of the bag.
He waves to the vendor and leaves.
'waves' is a friendly action with the hand.

checks.q: What fruit does the man buy?
opts: He picks up and buys one red apple.
He buys an apple, not a banana.
The fruit is a red apple, not an orange.
checks.why: The clip shows the man choosing and buying a red apple.

checks.q: What does the man give to the vendor?
opts: He hands a coin to the vendor to pay.
He gives money, not a book.
He gives a coin, not a flower.
checks.why: The man pays the vendor by giving a coin.
